vulnerability.title: Ensure All User Initialization Files Have Mode 0740 Or Less Permissive
vulnerability.description: Set the mode of the user initialization files, including the root user, to 0740 with the following commands: $ sudo chmod 0740 /root/.INIT_FILE $ sudo chmod 0740 /home/USER/.INIT_FILE
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: var_user_initialization_files_regex='' readarray -t interactive_users < <(awk -F: '$3==0 || $3>=1000 {print $1}' /etc/passwd) readarray -t interactive_users_home < <(awk -F: '$3==0 || $3>=1000 {print $6}' /etc/passwd) readarray -t interactive_users_shell < <(awk -F: '$3==0 || $3>=1000 {print $7}' /etc/passwd) USERS_IGNORED_REGEX='nobody|nfsnobody' for (( i=0; i<"${#interactive_users[@]}"; i++ )); do if ! grep -qP "$USERS_IGNORED_REGEX" <<< "${interactive_users[$i]}" && \ [ "${interactive_users_shell[$i]}" != "/sbin/nologin" ]; then readarray -t init_files < <(find "${interactive_users_home[$i]}" -maxdepth 1 \ -exec basename {} \; | grep -P "$var_user_initialization_files_regex") for file in "${init_files[@]}"; do chmod u-s,g-wxs,o= "${interactive_users_home[$i]}/$file" done fi done

triage_decision.reason: Changing user initialization file permissions to 0740 only restricts access; it does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage.

llm_verdict.message: . This remediates the OpenSCAP finding by ensuring no user initialization file is more permissive than 0740.

review_feedback: The plan only addresses the root user's initialization files and does not cover all users as required by the vulnerability.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan only addresses the root user's initialization files and does not cover all users as required by the vulnerability.
concerns: Only root's home directory is considered; other users' initialization files are left unchanged.
Using find without -print0 or handling of filenames with spaces/newlines could cause issues.
The plan does not verify that the mode is set to 0740 or more restrictive (e.g., using symbolic mode u-s,g-wxs,o=).
suggested_improvements: Iterate over all users with UID 0 or UID >= 1000 from /etc/passwd.
For each user's home directory, use find with -type f -name '.*' -exec chmod 0740 {} + to change permissions.
Consider using symbolic mode 'u-s,g-wxs,o=' to match the recommendation exactly.
Handle filenames safely with find -print0 and xargs -0 if needed.
